what_it_does: A single-file HTML/CSS slide mockup showing a fixed 1600x900 canvas with two light-toned cards, sampled color labels, metric boxes, fragment bullets, and a gold-wash summary band.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts.
Defines sampled color variables for gold, bronze, charcoal, body text, line, page, warm plate, cool plate, nested box, and gold-wash band.
Uses a fixed 1600px by 900px centered presentation canvas on a gray browser background.
Displays two side-by-side cards labeled Warm plate and Cool plate, each with metric rows and explanatory fragments.
Separates cards with faint borders, soft shadows, spacing, and light backgrounds rather than dark contrast.
Includes decorative elements: faint grid plan, triangle bullets, clock icon, hatched footer strip, and circular V mark.
Summary band uses a pale gold background with a masked diagonal hatch overlay.
standouts: Near-white surfaces are distinguished through small hue differences and labeled hex values.
The file explicitly frames separation as shadow, border, and gap rather than dark fill.
The summary band and card surfaces are tied to sampled color values in comments and visible labels.
conventions: Keeps all CSS and markup in one HTML file.
Uses CSS custom properties for palette values.
Uses absolute positioning for the slide frame and major sections.
Uses utility-like class names for visual roles such as card, statrow, box, band, and mark.
Uses pseudo-elements for decorative overlays and icon hands.
concerns: The layout is fixed at 1600x900 and has no responsive behavior.
The external Google Fonts dependency affects standalone rendering if the font request fails.
The clock icon is CSS-only and not exposed with semantic text.
Visible copy references source sampling but the file does not identify the source slides.
notes: A fixed presentation mockup documenting a sampled warm/cool ivory palette and soft separation treatment.
questions: What source slides were sampled for the color values?
Is the fixed 1600x900 canvas intended for export, presentation, or browser viewing?
Should the visible labels remain part of the final design or serve only as annotation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual file: it demonstrates true-tone sampled surfaces using two cards, labeled hex values, and a pale gold summary band.
intention_signals: It appears intended to document and visually test subtle sampled surface tones, showing how warm and cool plates can remain distinct through hue, border, shadow, and spacing.
novelty: The file centers on extremely close warm/cool off-white differences and makes the contrast logic explicit in both comments and visible annotations.
contribution: Contributes a palette-and-surface treatment for retaining sampled warm ivory, cool ivory, nested grey, and pale gold-wash surfaces with soft border-shadow separation and annotated metric-card structure.
summary_for_embedding: Single-file fixed-size HTML slide demonstrating sampled true-tone warm and cool card surfaces, metric boxes, soft separation, and a pale gold summary band.
intention_for_embedding: Created to test and communicate subtle source-sampled color treatments and how they separate through hue, shadow, border, and layout rather than dark contrast.
